NKJV.1: “Judge not, that you be not judged.
NKJV.2: For with what judgment you judge, you will be judged;  and with the measure you use, it will be measured back to you.
NKJV.3: And why do you look at the speck in your brother’s eye, but do not consider the plank in your own eye?
NKJV.4: Or how can you say to your brother, ‘Let me remove the speck from your eye’; and look, a plank is in your own eye?
NKJV.5: Hypocrite! First remove the plank from your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.
NKJV.6: “Do not give what is holy to the dogs; nor cast your pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you in pieces.
NKJV.7: “Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
NKJV.8: For  everyone who asks receives, and he who seeks finds, and to him who knocks it will be opened.
NKJV.9: Or what man is there among you who, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
NKJV.10: Or if he asks for a fish, will he give him a serpent?
NKJV.11: If you then,  being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask Him!
NKJV.12: Therefore,  whatever you want men to do to you, do also to them, for  this is the Law and the Prophets.
NKJV.13: “Enter by the narrow gate; for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and there are many who go in by it.
NKJV.14: Because narrow is the gate and difficult is the way which leads to life, and there are few who find it.
NKJV.15: “Beware of false prophets,  who come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly they are ravenous wolves.
NKJV.16: You will know them by their fruits.  Do men gather grapes from thornbushes or figs from thistles?
NKJV.17: Even so,  every good tree bears good fruit, but a bad tree bears bad fruit.
NKJV.18: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a bad tree bear good fruit.
NKJV.19: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
NKJV.20: Therefore by their fruits you will know them.
NKJV.21: “Not everyone who says to Me,  ‘Lord, Lord,’ shall enter the kingdom of heaven, but he who  does the will of My Father in heaven.
NKJV.22: Many will say to Me in that day, ‘Lord, Lord, have we  not prophesied in Your name, cast out demons in Your name, and done many wonders in Your name?’
NKJV.23: And  then I will declare to them, ‘I never knew you;  depart from Me, you who practice lawlessness!’
NKJV.24: “Therefore  whoever hears these sayings of Mine, and does them, I will liken him to a wise man who built his house on the rock:
NKJV.25: and the rain descended, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it did not fall, for it was founded on the rock.
NKJV.26: “But everyone who hears these sayings of Mine, and does not do them, will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand:
NKJV.27: and the rain descended, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it fell. And great was its fall.”
NKJV.28: And so it was, when Jesus had ended these sayings, that the people were astonished at His teaching,
NKJV.29: for He taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.

ESV.1: “Judge not, that you be not judged.
ESV.2: For with the judgment you pronounce you will be judged, and with the measure you use it will be measured to you.
ESV.3: Why do you see the speck that is in your brother's eye, but do not notice the log that is in your own eye?
ESV.4: Or how can you say to your brother, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ when there is the log in your own eye?
ESV.5: You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your brother's eye.
ESV.6: “Do not give dogs what is holy, and do not throw your pearls before pigs, lest they trample them underfoot and turn to attack you.
ESV.7: “Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
ESV.8: For everyone who asks receives, and the one who seeks finds, and to the one who knocks it will be opened.
ESV.9: Or which one of you, if his son asks him for bread, will give him a stone?
ESV.10: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a serpent?
ESV.11: If you then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
ESV.12: “So whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them, for this is the Law and the Prophets.
ESV.13: “Enter by the narrow gate. For the gate is wide and the way is easy  that leads to destruction, and those who enter by it are many.
ESV.14: For the gate is narrow and the way is hard that leads to life, and those who find it are few.
ESV.15: “Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep's clothing but inwardly are ravenous wolves.
ESV.16: You will recognize them by their fruits. Are grapes gathered from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?
ESV.17: So, every healthy tree bears good fruit, but the diseased tree bears bad fruit.
ESV.18: A healthy tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a diseased tree bear good fruit.
ESV.19: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
ESV.20: Thus you will recognize them by their fruits.
ESV.21: “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, but the one who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.
ESV.22: On that day many will say to me, ‘Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and cast out demons in your name, and do many mighty works in your name?’
ESV.23: And then will I declare to them, ‘I never knew you; depart from me, you workers of lawlessness.’
ESV.24: “Everyone then who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
ESV.25: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house, but it did not fall, because it had been founded on the rock.
ESV.26: And everyone who hears these words of mine and does not do them will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand.
ESV.27: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell, and great was the fall of it.”
ESV.28: And when Jesus finished these sayings, the crowds were astonished at his teaching,
ESV.29: for he was teaching them as one who had authority, and not as their scribes.

NASB.1: 'Do not judge so that you will not be judged.
NASB.2: 'For in the way you judge, you will be judged; and by your standard of measure, it will be measured to you.
NASB.3: 'Why do you look at the speck that is in your brother's eye, but do not notice the log that is in your own eye?
NASB.4: 'Or how can you say to your brother, 'Let me take the speck out of your eye,' and behold, the log is in your own eye?
NASB.5: 'You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your brother's eye.
NASB.6: 'Do not give what is holy to dogs, and do not throw your pearls before swine, or they will trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you to pieces.
NASB.7: 'Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
NASB.8: 'For everyone who asks receives, and he who seeks finds, and to him who knocks it will be opened.
NASB.9: 'Or what man is there among you who, when his son asks for a loaf, will give him a stone?
NASB.10: 'Or if he asks for a fish, he will not give him a snake, will he?
NASB.11: 'If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give what is good to those who ask Him!
NASB.12: 'In everything, therefore, treat people the same way you want them to treat you, for this is the Law and the Prophets.
NASB.13: 'Enter through the narrow gate; for the gate is wide and the way is broad that leads to destruction, and there are many who enter through it.
NASB.14: 'For the gate is small and the way is narrow that leads to life, and there are few who find it.
NASB.15: 'Beware of the false prophets, who come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly are ravenous wolves.
NASB.16: 'You will know them by their fruits. Grapes are not gathered from thorn [bushes] nor figs from thistles, are they?
NASB.17: 'So every good tree bears good fruit, but the bad tree bears bad fruit.
NASB.18: 'A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, nor can a bad tree produce good fruit.
NASB.19: 'Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
NASB.20: 'So then, you will know them by their fruits.
NASB.21: 'Not everyone who says to Me, 'Lord, Lord,' will enter the kingdom of heaven, but he who does the will of My Father who is in heaven [will enter].
NASB.22: 'Many will say to Me on that day, 'Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in Your name, and in Your name cast out demons, and in Your name perform many miracles?'
NASB.23: 'And then I will declare to them, 'I never knew you; DEPART FROM ME, YOU WHO PRACTICE LAWLESSNESS.'
NASB.24: 'Therefore everyone who hears these words of Mine and acts on them, may be compared to a wise man who built his house on the rock.
NASB.25: 'And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and slammed against that house; and [yet] it did not fall, for it had been founded on the rock.
NASB.26: 'Everyone who hears these words of Mine and does not act on them, will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand.
NASB.27: 'The rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and slammed against that house; and it fell-- and great was its fall.'
NASB.28: When Jesus had finished these words, the crowds were amazed at His teaching;
NASB.29: for He was teaching them as [one] having authority, and not as their scribes.

NLT.1: “Do not judge others, and you will not be judged.
NLT.2: For you will be treated as you treat others. The standard you use in judging is the standard by which you will be judged.
NLT.3: “And why worry about a speck in your friend’s eye when you have a log in your own?
NLT.4: How can you think of saying to your friend, ‘Let me help you get rid of that speck in your eye,’ when you can’t see past the log in your own eye?
NLT.5: Hypocrite! First get rid of the log in your own eye; then you will see well enough to deal with the speck in your friend’s eye.
NLT.6: “Don’t waste what is holy on people who are unholy. Don’t throw your pearls to pigs! They will trample the pearls, then turn and attack you.
NLT.7: “Keep on asking, and you will receive what you ask for. Keep on seeking, and you will find. Keep on knocking, and the door will be opened to you.
NLT.8: For everyone who asks, receives. Everyone who seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door will be opened.
NLT.9: “You parents — if your children ask for a loaf of bread, do you give them a stone instead?
NLT.10: Or if they ask for a fish, do you give them a snake? Of course not!
NLT.11: So if you sinful people know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good gifts to those who ask him.
NLT.12: “Do to others whatever you would like them to do to you. This is the essence of all that is taught in the law and the prophets.
NLT.13: “You can enter God’s Kingdom only through the narrow gate. The highway to hell is broad, and its gate is wide for the many who choose that way.
NLT.14: But the gateway to life is very narrow and the road is difficult, and only a few ever find it.
NLT.15: “Beware of false prophets who come disguised as harmless sheep but are really vicious wolves.
NLT.16: You can identify them by their fruit, that is, by the way they act. Can you pick grapes from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?
NLT.17: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
NLT.18: A good tree can’t produce bad fruit, and a bad tree can’t produce good fruit.
NLT.19: So every tree that does not produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
NLT.20: Yes, just as you can identify a tree by its fruit, so you can identify people by their actions.
NLT.21: “Not everyone who calls out to me, ‘Lord! Lord!’ will enter the Kingdom of Heaven. Only those who actually do the will of my Father in heaven will enter.
NLT.22: On judgment day many will say to me, ‘Lord! Lord! We prophesied in your name and cast out demons in your name and performed many miracles in your name.’
NLT.23: But I will reply, ‘I never knew you. Get away from me, you who break God’s laws.’
NLT.24: “Anyone who listens to my teaching and follows it is wise, like a person who builds a house on solid rock.
NLT.25: Though the rain comes in torrents and the floodwaters rise and the winds beat against that house, it won’t collapse because it is built on bedrock.
NLT.26: But anyone who hears my teaching and doesn’t obey it is foolish, like a person who builds a house on sand.
NLT.27: When the rains and floods come and the winds beat against that house, it will collapse with a mighty crash.”
NLT.28: When Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were amazed at his teaching,
NLT.29: for he taught with real authority — quite unlike their teachers of religious law.

WEB.1: “Don’t judge, so that you won’t be judged.
WEB.2: For with whatever judgment you judge, you will be judged; and with whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you.
WEB.3: Why do you see the speck that is in your brother’s eye, but don’t consider the beam that is in your own eye?
WEB.4: Or how will you tell your brother, ‘Let me remove the speck from your eye;’ and behold, the beam is in your own eye?
WEB.5: You hypocrite! First remove the beam out of your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck out of your brother’s eye.
WEB.6: “Don’t give that which is holy to the dogs, neither throw your pearls before the pigs, lest perhaps they trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you to pieces.
WEB.7: “Ask, and it will be given you. Seek, and you will find. Knock, and it will be opened for you.
WEB.8: For everyone who asks receives. He who seeks finds. To him who knocks it will be opened.
WEB.9: Or who is there among you, who, if his son asks him for bread, will give him a stone?
WEB.10: Or if he asks for a fish, who will give him a serpent?
WEB.11: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
WEB.12: Therefore whatever you desire for men to do to you, you shall also do to them; for this is the law and the prophets.
WEB.13: “Enter in by the narrow gate; for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many are those who enter in by it.
WEB.14: How  narrow is the gate, and restricted is the way that leads to life! Few are those who find it.
WEB.15: “Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly are ravening wolves.
WEB.16: By their fruits you will know them. Do you gather grapes from thorns, or figs from thistles?
WEB.17: Even so, every good tree produces good fruit; but the corrupt tree produces evil fruit.
WEB.18: A good tree can’t produce evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree produce good fruit.
WEB.19: Every tree that doesn’t grow good fruit is cut down, and thrown into the fire.
WEB.20: Therefore by their fruits you will know them.
WEB.21: Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter into the Kingdom of Heaven; but he who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.
WEB.22: Many will tell me in that day, ‘Lord, Lord, didn’t we prophesy in your name, in your name cast out demons, and in your name do many mighty works?’
WEB.23: Then I will tell them, ‘I never knew you. Depart from me, you who work iniquity.’
WEB.24: “Everyone therefore who hears these words of mine, and does them, I will liken him to a wise man, who built his house on a rock.
WEB.25: The rain came down, the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat on that house; and it didn’t fall, for it was founded on the rock.
WEB.26: Everyone who hears these words of mine, and doesn’t do them will be like a foolish man, who built his house on the sand.
WEB.27: The rain came down, the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat on that house; and it fell — and great was its fall.”
WEB.28: When Jesus had finished saying these things, the multitudes were astonished at his teaching,
WEB.29: for he taught them with authority, and not like the scribes.

CSB17.1: “Do not judge, so that you won’t be judged. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.2: For you will be judged by the same standard with which you judge others, and you will be measured by the same measure you use. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.3: Why do you look at the splinter in your brother’s eye but don’t notice the beam of wood in your own eye? <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.4: Or how can you say to your brother, ‘Let me take the splinter out of your eye,’ and look, there’s a beam of wood in your own eye?
CSB17.5: Hypocrite! First take the beam of wood out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brother’s eye.
CSB17.6: Don’t give what is holy to dogs or toss your pearls before pigs, <sup>ⓚ</sup>or they will trample them under their feet, turn, and tear you to pieces.
CSB17.7: “Ask, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and it will be given to you. <sup>ⓜ</sup> Seek, and you will find. Knock, and the door <sup>[1]</sup> will be opened to you.
CSB17.8: For everyone who asks receives, and the one who seeks finds, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and to the one who knocks, the door will be opened.
CSB17.9: Who among you, if his son asks him for bread, will give him a stone?
CSB17.10: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?
CSB17.11: If you then, who are evil, <sup>ⓐ</sup>know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask him.
CSB17.12: Therefore, whatever you want others to do for you, do also the same for them, for this is the Law and the Prophets. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.13: “Enter through the narrow gate. <sup>ⓒ</sup> For the gate is wide and the road broad that leads to destruction, <sup>ⓓ</sup> and there are many who go through it.
CSB17.14: How narrow is the gate and difficult the road that leads to life, and few find it.
CSB17.15: “Be on your guard against false prophets <sup>ⓔ</sup> who come to you in sheep’s <sup>ⓕ</sup> clothing <sup>ⓖ</sup> but inwardly are ravaging wolves. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.16: You’ll recognize them by their fruit. <sup>ⓘ</sup> Are grapes gathered from thornbushes or figs from thistles? <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.17: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, but a bad tree produces bad fruit.
CSB17.18: A good tree can’t produce bad fruit; neither can a bad tree produce good fruit.
CSB17.19: Every tree that doesn’t produce good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.20: So you’ll recognize them by their fruit. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.21: “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, <sup>ⓜ</sup> but only the one who does the will <sup>ⓝ</sup> of my Father in heaven. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.22: On that day many will say to me, ‘Lord, Lord, didn’t we prophesy in your name, drive out demons <sup>ⓑ</sup>in your name, and do many miracles in your name?’ <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.23: Then I will announce to them, ‘I never knew you. Depart from me, you lawbreakers!’ [2] [3] ⓓ
CSB17.24: “Therefore, <sup>ⓔ</sup> everyone who hears these words <sup>ⓕ</sup>of mine and acts on them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
CSB17.25: The rain fell, the rivers rose, and the winds blew and pounded that house. Yet it didn’t collapse, because its foundation was on the rock.
CSB17.26: But everyone who hears these words of mine and doesn’t act on them will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand.
CSB17.27: The rain fell, the rivers rose, the winds blew and pounded that house, and it collapsed. It collapsed with a great crash.”
CSB17.28: When Jesus had finished saying these things, <sup>ⓖ</sup> the crowds were astonished at his teaching, <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.29: because he was teaching them like one who had authority, and not like their scribes.

AMP.1: " Do not judge and criticize and condemn [others unfairly with an attitude of self-righteous superiority as though assuming the office of a judge], so that you will not be judged [unfairly].
AMP.2: For just as you [hypocritically] judge others [when you are sinful and unrepentant], so will you be judged; and in accordance with your standard of measure [used to pass out judgment], judgment will be measured to you.
AMP.3: Why do you look at the [insignificant] speck that is in your brother's eye, but do not notice and acknowledge the [egregious] log that is in your own eye?
AMP.4: Or how can you say to your brother, 'Let me get the speck out of your eye,' when there is a log in your own eye?
AMP.5: You hypocrite (play-actor, pretender), first get the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your brother's eye.
AMP.6: "Do not give that which is holy to dogs, and do not throw your pearls before pigs, for they will trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you to pieces.
AMP.7: " Ask and keep on asking and it will be given to you; seek and keep on seeking and you will find; knock and keep on knocking and the door will be opened to you.
AMP.8: For everyone who keeps on asking receives, and he who keeps on seeking finds, and to him who keeps on knocking, it will be opened.
AMP.9: Or what man is there among you who, if his son asks for bread, will [instead] give him a stone?
AMP.10: Or if he asks for a fish, will [instead] give him a snake?
AMP.11: If you then, evil (sinful by nature) as you are, know how to give good and advantageous gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven [perfect as He is] give what is good and advantageous to those who keep on asking Him.
AMP.12: "So then, in everything treat others the same way you want them to treat you, for this is [the essence of] the Law and the [writings of the] Prophets.
AMP.13: "Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad and easy to travel is the path that leads the way to destruction and eternal loss, and there are many who enter through it.
AMP.14: But small is the gate and narrow and difficult to travel is the path that leads the way to [everlasting] life, and there are few who find it.
AMP.15: "Beware of the false prophets, [teachers] who come to you dressed as sheep [appearing gentle and innocent], but inwardly are ravenous wolves.
AMP.16: By their fruit you will recognize them [that is, by their contrived doctrine and self-focus]. Do people pick grapes from thorn bushes or figs from thistles?
AMP.17: Even so, every healthy tree bears good fruit, but the unhealthy tree bears bad fruit.
AMP.18: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a bad tree bear good fruit.
AMP.19: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
AMP.20: Therefore, by their fruit you will recognize them [as false prophets].
AMP.21: "Not everyone who says to Me, 'Lord, Lord,' will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only he who does the will of My Father who is in heaven.
AMP.22: Many will say to Me on that day [when I judge them], 'Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in Your name, and driven out demons in Your name, and done many miracles in Your name?'
AMP.23: And then I will declare to them publicly, 'I never knew you; depart from Me [you are banished from My presence], you who act wickedly [disregarding My commands].'
AMP.24: "So everyone who hears these words of Mine and acts on them, will be like a wise man [a far-sighted, practical, and sensible man] who built his house on the rock.
AMP.25: And the rain fell, and the floods and torrents came, and the winds blew and slammed against that house; yet it did not fall, because it had been founded on the rock.
AMP.26: And everyone who hears these words of Mine and does not do them, will be like a foolish (stupid) man who built his house on the sand.
AMP.27: And the rain fell, and the floods and torrents came, and the winds blew and slammed against that house; and it fell—and great and complete was its fall."
AMP.28: When Jesus had finished [speaking] these words [on the mountain], the crowds were astonished and overwhelmed at His teaching;
AMP.29: for He was teaching them as one who had authority [to teach entirely of His own volition], and not as their scribes [who relied on others to confirm their authority].

MSG.1: "Don't pick on people, jump on their failures, criticize their faults—unless, of course, you want the same treatment.
MSG.2: That critical spirit has a way of boomeranging.
MSG.3: It's easy to see a smudge on your neighbor's face and be oblivious to the ugly sneer on your own.
MSG.4: Do you have the nerve to say, 'Let me wash your face for you,' when your own face is distorted by contempt?
MSG.5: It's this whole traveling road-show mentality all over again, playing a holier-than-thou part instead of just living your part. Wipe that ugly sneer off your own face, and you might be fit to offer a washcloth to your neighbor.
MSG.6: "Don't be flip with the sacred. Banter and silliness give no honor to God. Don't reduce holy mysteries to slogans. In trying to be relevant, you're only being cute and inviting sacrilege.
MSG.7: "Don't bargain with God. Be direct. Ask for what you need.
MSG.8: This isn't a cat-and-mouse, hide-and-seek game we're in.
MSG.9: If your child asks for bread, do you trick him with sawdust?
MSG.10: If he asks for fish, do you scare him with a live snake on his plate?
MSG.11: As bad as you are, you wouldn't think of such a thing. You're at least decent to your own children. So don't you think the God who conceived you in love will be even better?
MSG.12: "Here is a simple, rule-of-thumb guide for behavior: Ask yourself what you want people to do for you, then grab the initiative and do it for them. Add up God's Law and Prophets and this is what you get.
MSG.13: "Don't look for shortcuts to God. The market is flooded with surefire, easygoing formulas for a successful life that can be practiced in your spare time. Don't fall for that stuff, even though crowds of people do.
MSG.14: The way to life—to God!—is vigorous and requires total attention.
MSG.15: "Be wary of false preachers who smile a lot, dripping with practiced sincerity. Chances are they are out to rip you off some way or other. Don't be impressed with charisma; look for character.
MSG.16: Who preachers are is the main thing, not what they say. A genuine leader will never exploit your emotions or your pocketbook. These diseased trees with their bad apples are going to be chopped down and burned.
MSG.21: "Knowing the correct password—saying 'Master, Master,' for instance—isn't going to get you anywhere with me. What is required is serious obedience—doing what my Father wills.
MSG.22: I can see it now—at the Final Judgment thousands strutting up to me and saying, 'Master, we preached the Message, we bashed the demons, our God-sponsored projects had everyone talking.'
MSG.23: And do you know what I am going to say? 'You missed the boat. All you did was use me to make yourselves important. You don't impress me one bit. You're out of here.'
MSG.24: "These words I speak to you are not incidental additions to your life, homeowner improvements to your standard of living. They are foundational words, words to build a life on. If you work these words into your life, you are like a smart carpenter who built his house on solid rock.
MSG.25: Rain poured down, the river flooded, a tornado hit—but nothing moved that house. It was fixed to the rock.
MSG.26: "But if you just use my words in Bible studies and don't work them into your life, you are like a stupid carpenter who built his house on the sandy beach.
MSG.27: When a storm rolled in and the waves came up, it collapsed like a house of cards."
MSG.28: When Jesus concluded his address, the crowd burst into applause. They had never heard teaching like this.
MSG.29: It was apparent that he was living everything he was saying—quite a contrast to their religion teachers! This was the best teaching they had ever heard.

NIV.1:
Judging Others
“Do not judge, or you too will be judged.
NIV.2: For in the same way you judge others, you will be judged, and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
NIV.3: “Why do you look at the speck of sawdust in your brother’s eye and pay no attention to the plank in your own eye?
NIV.4: How can you say to your brother, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ when all the time there is a plank in your own eye?
NIV.5: You hypocrite, first take the plank out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.
NIV.6: “Do not give dogs what is sacred; do not throw your pearls to pigs. If you do, they may trample them under their feet, and then turn and tear you to pieces.
NIV.7:
Ask, Seek, Knock
“Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.
NIV.8: For everyone who asks receives; he who seeks finds; and to him who knocks, the door will be opened.
NIV.9: “Which of you, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
NIV.10: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?
NIV.11: If you, then, though you are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good gifts to those who ask him!
NIV.12: So in everything, do to others what you would have them do to you, for this sums up the Law and the Prophets.
NIV.13:
The Narrow and Wide Gates
“Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the road that leads to destruction, and many enter through it.
NIV.14: But small is the gate and narrow the road that leads to life, and only a few find it.
NIV.15:
A Tree and Its Fruit
“Watch out for false prophets. They come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly they are ferocious wolves.
NIV.16: By their fruit you will recognize them. Do people pick grapes from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?
NIV.17: Likewise every good tree bears good fruit, but a bad tree bears bad fruit.
NIV.18: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot bear good fruit.
NIV.19: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
NIV.20: Thus, by their fruit you will recognize them.
NIV.21: “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only he who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.
NIV.22: Many will say to me on that day, ‘Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and in your name drive out demons and perform many miracles?’
NIV.23: Then I will tell them plainly, ‘I never knew you. Away from me, you evildoers!’
NIV.24:
The Wise and Foolish Builders
“Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
NIV.25: The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house; yet it did not fall, because it had its foundation on the rock.
NIV.26: But everyone who hears these words of mine and does not put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
NIV.27: The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell with a great crash.”
NIV.28: When Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were amazed at his teaching,
NIV.29: because he taught as one who had authority, and not as their teachers of the law.

NRSVCE.1: “Do not judge, so that you may not be judged.
NRSVCE.2: For with the judgment you make you will be judged, and the measure you give will be the measure you get.
NRSVCE.3: Why do you see the speck in your neighbor’s eye, but do not notice the log in your own eye?
NRSVCE.4: Or how can you say to your neighbor, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ while the log is in your own eye?
NRSVCE.5: You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your neighbor’s eye.
NRSVCE.6: “Do not give what is holy to dogs; and do not throw your pearls before swine, or they will trample them under foot and turn and maul you.
NRSVCE.7: “Ask, and it will be given you; search, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened for you.
NRSVCE.8: For everyone who asks receives, and everyone who searches finds, and for everyone who knocks, the door will be opened.
NRSVCE.9: Is there anyone among you who, if your child asks for bread, will give a stone?
NRSVCE.10: Or if the child asks for a fish, will give a snake?
NRSVCE.11: If you then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
NRSVCE.12: “In everything do to others as you would have them do to you; for this is the law and the prophets.
NRSVCE.13: “Enter through the narrow gate; for the gate is wide and the road is easy that leads to destruction, and there are many who take it.
NRSVCE.14: For the gate is narrow and the road is hard that leads to life, and there are few who find it.
NRSVCE.15: “Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep’s clothing but inwardly are ravenous wolves.
NRSVCE.16: You will know them by their fruits. Are grapes gathered from thorns, or figs from thistles?
NRSVCE.17: In the same way, every good tree bears good fruit, but the bad tree bears bad fruit.
NRSVCE.18: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a bad tree bear good fruit.
NRSVCE.19: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
NRSVCE.20: Thus you will know them by their fruits.
NRSVCE.21: “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only the one who does the will of my Father in heaven.
NRSVCE.22: On that day many will say to me, ‘Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and cast out demons in your name, and do many deeds of power in your name?’
NRSVCE.23: Then I will declare to them, ‘I never knew you; go away from me, you evildoers.’
NRSVCE.24: “Everyone then who hears these words of mine and acts on them will be like a wise man who built his house on rock.
NRSVCE.25: The rain fell, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house, but it did not fall, because it had been founded on rock.
NRSVCE.26: And everyone who hears these words of mine and does not act on them will be like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
NRSVCE.27: The rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell — and great was its fall!”
NRSVCE.28: Now when Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were astounded at his teaching,
NRSVCE.29: for he taught them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.

NET.1: “Do not judge so that you will not be judged.
NET.2: For by the standard you judge you will be judged, and the measure you use will be the measure you receive.
NET.3: Why do you see the speck in your brother’s eye, but fail to see the beam of wood in your own?
NET.4: Or how can you say to your brother, ‘Let me remove the speck from your eye,’ while there is a beam in your own?
NET.5: You hypocrite! First remove the beam from your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.
NET.6: Do not give what is holy to dogs or throw your pearls before pigs; otherwise they will trample them under their feet and turn around and tear you to pieces.
NET.7: “Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened for you.
NET.8: For everyone who asks receives, and the one who seeks finds, and to the one who knocks, the door will be opened.
NET.9: Is there anyone among you who, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
NET.10: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?
NET.11: If you then, although you are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good gifts to those who ask him!
NET.12: In everything, treat others as you would want them to treat you, for this fulfills the law and the prophets.
NET.13: “Enter through the narrow gate, because the gate is wide and the way is spacious that leads to destruction, and there are many who enter through it.
NET.14: But the gate is narrow and the way is difficult that leads to life, and there are few who find it.
NET.15: “Watch out for false prophets, who come to you in sheep’s clothing but inwardly are voracious wolves.
NET.16: You will recognize them by their fruit. Grapes are not gathered from thorns or figs from thistles, are they?
NET.17: In the same way, every good tree bears good fruit, but the bad tree bears bad fruit.
NET.18: A good tree is not able to bear bad fruit, nor a bad tree to bear good fruit.
NET.19: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
NET.20: So then, you will recognize them by their fruit.
NET.21: “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter into the kingdom of heaven — only the one who does the will of my Father in heaven.
NET.22: On that day, many will say to me, ‘Lord, Lord, didn’t we prophesy in your name, and in your name cast out demons and do many powerful deeds?’
NET.23: Then I will declare to them, ‘I never knew you. Go away from me, you lawbreakers!’
NET.24: “Everyone who hears these words of mine and does them is like a wise man who built his house on rock.
NET.25: The rain fell, the flood came, and the winds beat against that house, but it did not collapse because it had been founded on rock.
NET.26: Everyone who hears these words of mine and does not do them is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
NET.27: The rain fell, the flood came, and the winds beat against that house, and it collapsed; it was utterly destroyed!”
NET.28: When Jesus finished saying these things, the crowds were amazed by his teaching,
NET.29: because he taught them like one who had authority, not like their experts in the law.
